confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_48fdf40f-0def-4a7e-ba40-5809ea82fae3 read back as promoted

evidence: operator-surfaces/input-submit-strategies.json now admits operator-surface message passing for Bob, narada.architect, and narada.builder, but this is not surfaced as a discoverable capability announcement.
Canonical inbox can carry observations/proposals, but it does not distinguish capability announcements as a typed envelope or durable registry entry.
Site config capabilities describe broad site affordances, not newly learned operation-level capabilities with evidence and constraints.
proposal: Define CapabilityAnnouncement as a typed envelope and/or durable Site registry projection with fields: capability id, summary, owner Site, authority scope, usable-by identities/roles, entrypoints, prerequisites, evidence, constraints, safety posture, adoption posture, and supersession/versioning.
Allow a Site to publish a capability announcement to relevant Sites through canonical inbox publication, so agents can discover and use admitted capabilities without relying on chat memory.

Use operator_surface_message_passing as the first test case: announce Send-Os.ps1 / Send-OperatorSurfaceInput.ps1, required runtime identity binding, known submit strategy requirement, no-secrets constraint, and observed successful sends.
payload.recommendation: Treat as Narada proper architecture work for capability propagation and Site-to-Site operational learning.
payload.summary: Narada currently has capability declarations, KB documentation, inbox observations, and published inbox envelopes, but no first-class construct for a Site to announce a newly admitted operational capability with scope, entrypoints, evidence, constraints, and adoption posture. The operator-surface message-passing capability exposed this gap: other Narada agents can use it only if they infer it from local files, chat, or ad hoc nudges.
payload.title: Add typed capability announcement construct
